reword this please •	The contractor is to verify the location of all existing services within the Waves of History site prior to commencement of demolition and construction. Damages incurred shall be repaired by the contractor or relevant authority at the direction of the superintendent at the contractors expense.

Before starting demolition and construction at the Waves of History site, the contractor must confirm the precise location of all existing services. If any damages occur, the contractor is responsible for repairing them or compensating the superintendent to arrange repairs through the relevant authority at the contractor's cost.
reword please •	Ensure that barriers, signage and pedestrian safety measures are put in place before work commences, that comply with the approved pm & tm plan."

Prior to commencing work, it is essential to implement proper barriers, signage, and pedestrian safety measures in accordance with the approved Plan of Management (PM) and Traffic Management (TM) plan.

•	The contractor shall confine all works within the extent of works boundary and take precautions to protect structures, underground infrastructure from services and granite stone wall, turf and vegetation from any damage during demolition.

During the demolition process, the contractor is responsible for confining all works within the designated extent of works boundary. Additionally, they must take necessary precautions to safeguard structures, underground infrastructure, such as services and the granite stone wall, as well as preserve the integrity of the turf and vegetation by preventing any damage.

•	All cost for damage by contractor and immediate repair to adjacent property shall be completed by qualified persons at the contractor’s expense. Where dust measures are inadequate, and dust is evident, the superintendent shall order the contractor to provide dust proof screens and covers and protect surroundings and finishes and immediate environment from dust.

The contractor is accountable for any damages caused and must arrange for the immediate repair of adjacent properties by qualified individuals at their own expense. In cases where the dust control measures are deemed insufficient and dust becomes noticeable, the superintendent has the authority to instruct the contractor to employ dustproof screens and covers. The contractor is further obligated to protect the surroundings, finishes, and immediate environment from dust.

Grubbing: Grub out roots over 75 mm diameter to a minimum depth of 500 mm below subgrade under embankments or existing paving, or 300 mm below finished surface in unpaved areas. Backfill holes remaining after grubbing with sand material to prevent ponding of water. Compact the material to the relative density of the existing adjacent ground material.

During the grubbing process, roots that are 75 mm or larger in diameter should be completely removed. This removal should extend to a minimum depth of 500 mm below the subgrade if it is beneath embankments or existing paving. In unpaved areas, the roots should be removed to a depth of 300 mm below the finished surface. After grubbing, any resulting holes should be backfilled with sand material to prevent water from collecting. The backfilled material should then be compacted to achieve a relative density that matches the existing adjacent ground material.

The project manager is to oversee the operations on-site, ensuring that the project progresses as per the approved plans and specifications. Coordination and supervision of construction activities to be undertaken by contractors to ensure the highest standards of quality and safety.

The project manager is responsible for the overall supervision and management of on-site operations to ensure that the project is carried out in accordance with the approved plans and specifications. This includes coordinating and overseeing the construction activities performed by contractors to uphold the highest standards of quality and safety.

A control plan with control measures is to be implemented. This involves the preparation of a soil erosion and sediment control plan to protect natural stormwater drainage systems from sedimentation going into the Brisbane River. Management principles to aid in preventing erosion and sediment control are in accordance with the 'Best Practice Erosion And Sediment Control' manual, published by the International Erosion Control Association (IECA)

To protect the natural stormwater drainage systems from sedimentation that could potentially impact the Brisbane River, it is crucial to implement a control plan with appropriate control measures. Part of this plan involves the development of a soil erosion and sediment control plan. The principles and practices to prevent erosion and manage sediment control should align with the guidelines outlined in the 'Best Practice Erosion And Sediment Control' manual published by the International Erosion Control Association (IECA). These measures aim to ensure the effective management of erosion and sediment to safeguard the water quality of the Brisbane River.

please make this super concise "During the grubbing process, roots that are 75 mm or larger in diameter should be completely removed. This removal should extend to a minimum depth of 500 mm below the subgrade if it is beneath embankments or existing paving. In unpaved areas, the roots should be removed to a depth of 300 mm below the finished surface. After grubbing, any resulting holes should be backfilled with sand material to prevent water from collecting. The backfilled material should then be compacted to achieve a relative density that matches the existing adjacent ground material. All debris including slabs, pavings, and foundations on the surface must be removed. "

During grubbing, remove roots with a diameter of 75 mm or greater. For areas beneath embankments or existing paving, remove roots to a depth of at least 500 mm below the subgrade. In unpaved areas, remove roots to a depth of 300 mm below the finished surface. Backfill resulting holes with sand material to prevent water accumulation and compact it to match the relative density of adjacent ground material. Remove all surface debris, including slabs, pavings, and foundations.

this too please "•	All site preparation to be in accordance with geotechnical report and documentation. 
•	Site conditions that would involve giving notice and obtaining instructions before commencing construction would include bad ground, rock, discrepancy in expected conditions, and topsoil more than 100mm deep. 
•	Earth reinforcement around batters and revetment wall to be implemented through the use of facing units and reinforcing anchors 
•	Trees to be retained and protected, such as existing FICUS benjamina trees, to be in accordance with AS 4970-2009 with displaying of signs at each entrance of site in accordance with AS 1744. 
•	All use of machinery including machinery to be utilised within the Brisbane River and within the remaining site shall be approved & conditioned by the superintendent prior to commencement.
•	The contractor is to verify the location of all existing services within the Waves of History site prior to commencement of demolition and construction. Damages incurred shall be repaired by the contractor or relevant authority at the direction of the superintendent at the contractor’s expense.
•	Before starting demolition and construction at the Waves of History site, the contractor must confirm the precise location of all existing services with surveyor. If any damages occur, the contractor is responsible for repairing them or compensating the superintendent to arrange repairs through the relevant authority at the contractor’s cost.
•	Dial Before You Dig (DBYD) investigations are to be undertaken and documentation is to be kept onsite. 
•	Unless if otherwise advised, all topsoil and excavated material must be continually removed from site as work proceeds to ensure safety and management of works. 
•	Additionally, a waste management control plan, ground contamination plan, weed management control plan, air quality control plan, water quality control plan, noise and vibration control plan, flora and fauna control plan, and a cultural and aboriginal heritage plan must be implemented as part of the Environmental Management Plan. 
"

• Site preparation must adhere to the geotechnical report and accompanying documentation.
• Notice and instructions should be sought before commencing construction in the presence of challenging site conditions such as bad ground, rock, unexpected discrepancies, and topsoil exceeding a depth of 100mm.
• Earth reinforcement for batters and revetment wall to be achieved through the use of facing units and reinforcing anchors.
• AS 4970-2009 guidelines must be followed for the retention and protection of trees, including existing FICUS benjamina trees. Signs indicating compliance with AS 1744 must be displayed at each site entrance.
• Approval and conditioning from the superintendent is required prior to using machinery within the Brisbane River or on the remaining site.
• The contractor is responsible for verifying the location of existing services within the Waves of History site before commencing demolition and construction. Any damages incurred should be repaired by the contractor or relevant authority, as directed by the superintendent.
• The contractor should also consult with a surveyor to confirm the precise location of existing services. Damages are the responsibility of the contractor, who may need to compensate the superintendent for repairs arranged through the relevant authority.
• Dial Before You Dig (DBYD) investigations are mandatory, with all accompanying documentation kept onsite.
• Unless otherwise advised, topsoil and excavated material must be continuously removed from the site for safety and efficient project management.
• Various plans, including waste management, ground contamination, weed management, air quality control, water quality control, noise and vibration control, flora and fauna control, as well as cultural and aboriginal heritage plans, must be implemented alongside the Environmental Management Plan.

reword plz ": Prepare the ground surface before placing fill (including topsoil fill), ground slabs or load bearing elements to AS 3798 clause 6.1.5. Remove material that inhibits or prevents satisfactory placement of fill layers, loose material, debris and organic matter.
Foundation preparation: To AS 3798 clause 6.1.7.
Compaction: Compact the ground exposed after stripping or excavation, to a minimum depth of 150 mm, to the minimum relative compaction in AS 3798 Table 5.1.
"

- Before placing fill, ground slabs, or load-bearing elements, prepare the ground surface according to AS 3798 clause 6.1.5. This includes removing any material that hinders or obstructs the proper placement of fill layers, as well as loose material, debris, and organic matter.
- Follow the foundation preparation guidelines outlined in AS 3798 clause 6.1.7.
- Compact the exposed ground after stripping or excavation to a minimum depth of 150 mm, achieving the minimum relative compaction specified in AS 3798 Table 5.1.
